question_title: Will HBAN's market close price on 2026-03-13 be higher than its market close price on 2026-03-04?
summary: HBAN is in a ~14% drawdown from its 52-week high with a recent sharp single-day drop (-4.2% on Feb 27). The conditional drawdown base rate from 704 historical events shows 54% probability of positive 7-day returns when in 10%+ drawdown — a mild mean-reversion edge. The shock rebound signal (82.4% positive over 10 days) is compelling but based on only 17 events and the bounce hasn't materialized 3 days in, weakening it. Recent 30-day momentum is strongly negative (-0.25%/day), and the broader S&P 500 is also showing mild weakness. Monte Carlo simulations range from 40% (using recent bearish drift) to 50% (zero drift). Synthesizing: the conditional drawdown base rate provides the strongest anchor at 54%, slightly offset by bearish momentum and market headwinds, landing at ~53%.

factors.description: Unconditional 7-day stock return base rate is approximately 50%
HBAN in ~14% drawdown from 52w high; conditional base rate shows 54% positive over 7 days from 704 historical events
Shock rebound signal: Feb 27 had -4.2% single-day drop; historically 82.4% positive over 10 days (17 events). 3 days passed without bounce, weakening signal
Recent 30-day momentum is strongly negative (-0.25%/day mean return), persistent selling pressure
S&P 500 showing mild weakness (-2.7% from 52w high, negative trailing returns), market headwind

reasoning: The Monte Carlo using recent 30-day returns (which have a negative mean) gives ~40%. But the recent period has been very bearish for HBAN — let me also check with a longer lookback and the shock alert data.
